PRs missing backports.title: Docs: fill in descriptor C API docs
PRs missing backports.body:
This documents several missing entries in `Doc/c-api/descriptor.rst`.

Changes in this PR:
- document `PyDescr_NewGetSet`
- document `PyDescr_NewMember`
- document `PyDescr_NewMethod`
- document `PyDescr_NewWrapper`
- document `PyDescr_NewClassMethod`
- document `PyWrapper_New`
- fix nearby Python type references for descriptor objects so they match the actual `types` module names

This is a docs-only change.

<!-- readthedocs-preview cpython-previews start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://cpython-previews--146644.org.readthedocs.build/

<!-- readthedocs-preview cpython-previews end -->

PRs missing backports.title: Fix minor typos in unicode.rst
PRs missing backports.body:
This PR fixes minor typos in Doc/c-api/unicode.rst. Fixes the below statement:

```
The return type is now ``const char *`` rather of ``char *``
```
as
```
The return type is now ``const char *`` rather than ``char *``
```
Also makes the word "written character" as "written characters" in:
```
Return the number of written character, or return ``-1`` and raise an
exception on error.
```

PRs missing backports.title: gh-149144: Use decodeURIComponent() for UTF-8 support in js_output()
PRs missing backports.body:
Related to #90309. Fixes a bug in the security fix https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/148848 

The JavaScript function `atob()` only outputs strings in `latin-1`, not in `utf-8`. `decodeURIComponent` outputs to UTF-8. Tested in Firefox and Chrome and the function [`decodeURIComponent` is supported by IE 5.5+](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mdn/browser-compat-data/refs/heads/main/javascript/builtins/globals.json#:~:text=decodeURIComponent).

Python:

```pycon
>>> import urllib.parse as u
>>> u.quote('Ł/😀', safe='', encoding='utf-8')
'%C5%81%2F%F0%9F%98%80'
```

JavaScript (Firefox, Chrome):

```
>>> decodeURIComponent("%C5%81%2F%F0%9F%98%80")
<<< "Ł/😀" 
```

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-149144 -->
* Issue: gh-149144
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: [3.14] gh-142183: Cache one datachunk per tstate to prevent alloc/dealloc thrashing (GH-145789)
PRs missing backports.body:
Cache one datachunk per tstate to prevent alloc/dealloc thrashing when repeatedly hitting the same call depth at exactly the wrong boundary.

---------
(cherry picked from commit 706fd4ec08acbf1b1def3630017ebe55d224adfa)

Co-authored-by: T. Wouters <thomas@python.org>
Co-authored-by: blurb-it[bot] <43283697+blurb-it[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-130094: Fix race conditions in `importlib`
PRs missing backports.body:
Entries may be added or removed from `sys.meta_path` concurrently. For example, setuptools temporarily adds and removes the `distutils` finder from the beginning of the list. The local copy ensures that we don't skip over any entries.

Some packages modify `sys.modules` during import. For example, `collections` inserts the entry for `collections.abc`  into `sys.modules` during import. We need to ensure that we re-check `sys.modules` *after* the parent module is fully initialized.


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-130094 -->
* Issue: gh-130094
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

PRs missing backports.title: gh-146333: Fix quadratic regex backtracking in configparser option parsing
PRs missing backports.body:
The `_OPT_TMPL` and `_OPT_NV_TMPL` regexes have quadratic backtracking when a line contains many spaces between non-delimiter characters. The lazy `.*?` in the option group and the `\s*` before the delimiter overlap on whitespace, so the engine tries every possible split point.

The fix removes `\s*` before the delimiter. This is safe because the option name is already stripped via `.rstrip()` in `_handle_option` (line 1160), and the value is stripped via `.strip()` (line 1169).

Before: `x` + 40000 spaces + `y` takes ~86 seconds
After: ~0.004 seconds

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-146333 -->
* Issue: gh-146333
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->
